Identification.LongTitle: An Act to implement the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership between Canada, Australia, Brunei, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnam
Identification.ShortTitle: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act
Identification.RunningHead: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act to implement the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership between Canada, Australia, Brunei, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnamâ.
This enactment implements the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership, done at Santiago on March 8, 2018.

The general provisions of the enactment set out rules of interpretation and specify that no recourse is to be taken on the basis of sections 9 to 13 or any order made under those sections, or on the basis of the provisions of the Agreement, without the consent of the Attorney General of Canada.

Part 1 approves the Agreement, provides for the payment by Canada of its share of the expenditures associated with the operation of the institutional and administrative aspects of the Agreement and gives the Governor in Council the power to make orders in accordance with the Agreement.
Part 2 amends certain Acts to bring them into conformity with Canadaâs obligations under the Agreement.
Part 3 contains coordinating amendments and the coming into force provision.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to implement the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership between Canada, Australia, Brunei, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnam

Text.XRefExternal: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act

TableOfProvisions.Text: Non-application of Act and Agreement to water
Causes of action under sections 9 to 13

Heading.TitleText: Approval and Representation on the Commission

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Coordinating Amendments and Coming into Force

Provision.Text: Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Agreementâmeans the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership between Canada, Australia, Brunei, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnam, done at Santiago on March 8, 2018.â(Accord)
Commissionâmeans the Commission established under the Agreement and whose powers, functions and duties are set out in Chapter 27 of the TPP.â(Commission)

federal lawâmeans the whole or any portion of an Act of Parliament or a regulation, order or other instrument issued, made or established in the exercise of a power conferred by or under an Act of Parliament.â(texte lÃ©gislatif fÃ©dÃ©ral)
Ministerâmeans the Minister for International Trade.â(ministre)
TPPâmeans the Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement as incorporated by reference into the Agreement by Article 1 of the Agreement. (PTP)

Section.Text: For greater certainty, this Act and any federal law that implements a provision of the Agreement or fulfils an obligation of the Government of Canada under the Agreement is to be interpreted in a manner consistent with the Agreement.

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application of Act and Agreement to water
Section.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in this Act or the Agreement applies to natural surface or ground water in liquid, gaseous or solid state.

For greater certainty, nothing in this Act, by specific mention or omission, is to be construed to affect in any manner the right of Parliament to enact legislation to implement any provision of the Agreement or fulfil any of the obligations of the Government of Canada under the Agreement.
This Act is binding on Her Majesty in right of Canada.
The purpose of this Act is to implement the Agreement, the objectives of which, as elaborated more specifically through its provisions, are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish a free trade area in accordance with the Agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: promote, through the expansion of reciprocal trade, the harmonious development of the economic relations between Canada and other parties to the Agreement in order to create opportunities for economic development;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: promote conditions of fair competition affecting trade between Canada and other parties to the Agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: substantially increase investment opportunities between Canada and other parties to the Agreement, while preserving the right of each of the parties to regulate to achieve legitimate policy goals;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: eliminate barriers to trade in goods and services in order to contribute to the harmonious development and expansion of world and regional trade, and seek to ensure that the benefits and opportunities created by the Agreement are widely shared;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: support the growth and development of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises by enhancing their ability to participate in and benefit from the opportunities created by trade;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: enhance and enforce environmental laws and regulations and strengthen cooperation between Canada and other parties to the Agreement on environmental matters;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: protect, enhance and enforce basic workersâ rights, strengthen cooperation on labour matters, and build on the respective international commitments of Canada and other parties to the Agreement on labour matters; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: recognise the importance of promoting corporate social responsibility, cultural identity and diversity, gender equality, the rights of Indigenous peoples, inclusive trade and sustainable development.

Section.MarginalNote: Causes of action under sections 9 to 13

Subsection.Text: No person has any cause of action and no proceedings of any kind are to be taken, without the consent of the Attorney General of Canada, to enforce or determine any right or obligation that is claimed or arises solely under or by virtue of sections 9 to 13 or an order made under those sections.

No person has any cause of action and no proceedings of any kind are to be taken, without the consent of the Attorney General of Canada, to enforce or determine any right or obligation that is claimed or arises solely under or by virtue of the Agreement.
Subsection (2) does not apply with respect to causes of action arising out of, and proceedings taken under, Section B of Chapter 9 or Article 11.22 of the TPP.

Heading.TitleText: Approval and Representation on the Commission

Section.Text: The Agreement is approved.
The Minister is the principal representative of Canada on the Commission.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: appoint a panellist in accordance with Article 28.9 of the TPP or establish a list of individuals who may serve as panellists in accordance with paragraph 9 of Article 28.11 of the TPP;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: propose the names of individuals for the roster referred to in Article 28.11 of the TPP; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: appoint an arbitrator in accordance with Section B of Chapter 9 of the TPP.

Subsection.Text: The Minister of Labour may act as Canadaâs senior governmental representative on the Labour Council referred to in Article 19.12 of the TPP or may designate that representative.

Section.Text: The Government of Canada is to pay its appropriate share of the aggregate of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any expenditures incurred by or on behalf of the Commission,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the general expenses incurred by the committees, working groups and other bodies established under the Agreement and the remuneration and expenses payable to representatives on the Commission and those committees and to members of those working groups and other bodies, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the expenses incurred by panels and arbitration tribunals established under the Agreement and the remuneration and expenses payable to the panellists on those panels, to arbitrators and to any experts retained by those panels or arbitration tribunals.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, for the purpose of suspending benefits in accordance with Article 28.20 of the TPP, by order, do any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspend rights or privileges granted by Canada to another party to the Agreement or to goods, service suppliers, investors or investments of investors of that party under the Agreement or any federal law;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: modify or suspend the application of any federal law, with respect to a party to the Agreement other than Canada or to goods, service suppliers, investors or investments of investors of that party;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: extend the application of any federal law to a party to the Agreement other than Canada or to goods, service suppliers, investors or investments of investors of that party; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: take any other measure that the Governor in Council considers necessary.

Subsection.Text: Unless repealed, an order made under subsection (1) has effect for the period specified in the order.
The definition free trade partner in subsection 2(1) of the Export and Import Permits Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.1):

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: CPTPP country,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: CPTPPâhas the same meaning as Agreement in section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act;â(PTPGP)
CPTPP countryâhas the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff;â(pays PTPGP)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(2) of the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCPTPP countryâ in the list of countries.

Section.Text: Section 6.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: The Minister, in relation to an allocation method established under paragraph (2)(a) or an import allocation issued under paragraph (2)(b), may accept payments and may receive any securities specified by the Minister.

Section.Text: Schedule VII to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Item.Text: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership between Canada, Australia, Brunei, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnam, done at Santiago on March 8, 2018.

Section.Text: Subsection 53(1) of the Trade-marks Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Where a court is satisfied, on application of any interested person, that any registered trade-mark, any trade-mark that is confusing with a registered trade-mark or any trade-name has been applied to any goods that have been imported into Canada or are about to be distributed in Canada in such a manner that the distribution of the goods would be contrary to this Act, or that any indication of a place of origin has been unlawfully applied to any goods, the court may make an order for the interim custody of the goods, pending a final determination of the legality of their importation or distribution in an action commenced within such time as is prescribed by the order.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 53.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Where a court is satisfied, on application by the owner of a registered trade-mark, that any goods to which the registered trade-mark or a trade-mark that is confusing with the registered trade-mark has been applied are about to be imported into Canada or have been imported into Canada but have not yet been released, and that the distribution of the goods in Canada would be contrary to this Act, the court may make an order

Section.Text: The schedule to the Investment Canada Act is amended by adding, at the end of column 1, a reference to âAgreement within the meaning of section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Actâ and a corresponding reference to âArticle 1.3 of the TPP within the meaning of section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Actâ in column 2.
Subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: CPTPPâhas the same meaning as Agreement in section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act;â(PTPGP)
CPTPP countryâhas the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff;â(pays PTPGP)

Section.Text: Section 35.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Certificate of origin completed by importer
Subsection.Text: If an importer of goods for which preferential tariff treatment under the CPTPP will be claimed is the person who certifies that the goods meet the rules of origin set out in, or contemplated by, the CPTPP, the importer shall do so in writing, in the prescribed form and containing the prescribed information, and on the basis of supporting documents that the importer has or supporting documents that are provided by the exporter or producer.

Section.Text: Subsection 42.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an exporter, producer or prescribed importer of goods that are subject to a verification of origin under paragraph (1)(a) fails to comply with the prescribed requirements or, in the case of a verification of origin under subparagraph (1)(a)(i), does not consent to the verification of origin in the prescribed manner and within the prescribed time, preferential tariff treatment under a free trade agreement, other than a free trade agreement referred to in subsection (1.1), may be denied or withdrawn from the goods.

Section.Text: Subsection 42.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On completion of a verification of origin under paragraph 42.1(1)(a), an officer designated under subsection 42.1(1) shall provide the exporter, producer or prescribed importer whose goods are subject to the verification of origin with a statement as to whether the goods are eligible, under the Customs Tariff, for the preferential tariff treatment that was claimed.

Section.Text: Subsection 42.4(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Denial or withdrawal of benefitâ¯ââ¯specified countries
Subsection.Text: Despite section 24 of the Customs Tariff, the Minister may, subject to any prescribed conditions, deny or withdraw preferential tariff treatment under an agreement set out in column 1 of Part 2 of the schedule in respect of goods for which that treatment is claimed if the exporter, producer or prescribed importer of the goods has made false representations that identical goods exported, produced or imported by that exporter, producer or prescribed importer and for which that treatment was claimed were eligible for that treatment.
Section 97.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If an exporter or producer of goods for which preferential tariff treatment under the CPTPP will be claimed in accordance with the laws of a CPTPP country is the person who certifies that the goods meet the rules of origin set out in, or contemplated by, the CPTPP, the exporter or producer shall do so in writing, in the prescribed form and containing the prescribed information, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an exporter, on the basis of supporting information that the exporter has or by relying on supporting information that the producer has; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a producer, on the basis of supporting information that the producer has.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 97.1(2) and (3) of the English version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provision of copy of Certificate of Origin
Subsection.Text: Every exporter or producer of goods who, for the purpose of enabling any person to comply with the applicable laws relating to customs of a free trade partner, completes and signs a certificate in accordance with subsection (1) or (1.1) shall, at the request of an officer, provide the officer with a copy of the certificate.

A person who has completed and signed a certificate in accordance with subsection (1) or (1.1) and who has reason to believe that it contains incorrect information shall immediately notify each person and CPTPP country to whom the certificate was given of the correct information.

Section.Text: Subsection 97.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person who exports goods or causes them to be exported for sale or for any industrial, occupational, commercial, institutional or other like use or any other use that may be prescribed, and every other person who has completed and signed a certificate in accordance with subsection 97.1(1) or (1.1), shall keep at the personâs place of business in Canada or at any other place that may be designated by the Minister any records in respect of those goods in the manner and for the period that may be prescribed and shall, if an officer requests, make them available to the officer, within the time specified by the officer, and answer any questions asked by the officer in respect of the records.

Section.Text: Part 1 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCPTPP countryâ in column 1, a corresponding reference to âCPTPPâ in column 2 and a corresponding reference to âCPTPP tariff rates of customs duty under the Customs Tariffâ in column 3.

Part 2 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCPTPPâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âparagraph 12 of Article 3.27 of the Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement as incorporated by reference into the Agreement by Article 1â in column 2.
Part 4 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCPTPP countryâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âCPTPPâ in column 2.

Part 5 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCPTPPâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âChapters 3 to 5 of the Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement as incorporated by reference into the Agreement by Article 1â in column 2.

Schedule 2 to the Commercial Arbitration Act is amended by adding, at the end of column 1, a reference to âArticle 9.19 of the Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement as incorporated by reference into the Agreement by Article 1â and a corresponding reference to âComprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership between Canada, Australia, Brunei, Chile, Japan, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Peru, Singapore and Vietnam, done at Santiago on March 8, 2018â in column 2.

Subsection.Text: The definition textile and apparel goods in subsection 2(1) of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: textile and apparel goodsâmeans the textile and apparel goods set out in Appendix 1.1 of Annex 300-B of the Agreement, in Appendix 1.1 of Annex C-00-B of the CCFTA, in Appendix III.1.1.1 of Annex III.1 of the CCRFTA, in section 1 of Annex 3.1 of the CHFTA or in Annex 4-A or Appendix 1 to Annex 4-A of the TPP, as the case may be;â(produits textiles et vÃªtements)

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.6):
In this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: CPTPP has the meaning assigned by the definition Agreement in section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: TPP has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: CPTPP country has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: CPTPP tariff has the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(5) of the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCPTPP countryâ in the list of countries.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 19.0192:

Subsection.Text: In this section, principal cause means, in respect of a serious injury or threat of a serious injury, an important cause that is no less important than any other cause of the serious injury or threat.

The Tribunal shall inquire into and report to the Governor in Council on the question whether goods of a CPTPP country, alone or together with the imported goods of other CPTPP countries, that are entitled to the benefit of a CPTPP tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a principal cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods, if the Governor in Council, on the recommendation of the Minister, refers the question to it for an inquiry and report.
The Tribunal shall conduct an inquiry under subsection (2) and prepare its report in accordance with the terms of reference established by the Governor in Council or the Minister, as the case may be.

The Minister shall cause a copy of each report submitted to the Governor in Council or the Minister to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the report is so submitted.
The Tribunal shall cause notice of the submission of a report to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: Section 23 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.081):

Subsection.Text: Any domestic producer of goods that are like or directly competitive with any goods being imported into Canada and that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff, or any person or association acting on behalf of such a domestic producer, may file a written complaint with the Tribunal alleging that, as a result of that entitlement, the imported goods of a CPTPP country, alone or together with the imported goods of other CPTPP countries, are being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to the domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Filing of complaintâ¯ââ¯textile and apparel goods
Subsection.Text: Any domestic producer of any textile and apparel goods that are like or directly competitive with any textile and apparel goods being imported into Canada and that are entitled under the Customs Tariff to a CPTPP tariff, or any person or association acting on behalf of such a domestic producer, may file a written complaint with the Tribunal alleging that, as a result of that entitlement, the imported goods of a CPTPP country, alone or together with the imported goods of other CPTPP countries are being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to the domestic market in Canada for the goods, and under such conditions as to cause serious damage, or actual threat thereof, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive textile or apparel goods.

Section.Text: Subsection 25(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Where the Tribunal determines under subsection (1) that a complaint is properly documented, it shall forthwith

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notify the complainant in writing that the complaint is properly documented;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: notify each other interested party in writing of the receipt of the complaint and that the complaint is properly documented; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.03), (1.06), (1.08), (1.083), (1.09), (1.091), (1.092) or (1.096), send to the Minister a copy of the complaint and the information examined by the Tribunal in making its determination.

Section.Text: Paragraph 26(1)(a) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (i.81):

Subparagraph.Label: (i.82)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.082), the goods that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff, taken individually for each CPTPP country concerned, are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a cause, when considered collectively, of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods,

Subparagraph.Label: (i.83)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.083), the textile and apparel goods that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff are as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to the market in Canada for the goods, and under such conditions as to cause serious damage, or actual threat thereof, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive textile and apparel goods,

Section.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.81):

Paragraph.Label: (a.82)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.082), the goods that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff, taken individually for each CPTPP country concerned, are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities and under such conditions as to constitute a principal cause, when considered collectively, of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods;

Paragraph.Label: (a.83)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.083), the textile and apparel goods that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to the market in Canada for the goods, and under such conditions as to cause serious damage, or actual threat thereof, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive textile and apparel goods;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 30.03(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Tribunal shall cause to be published in the Canada Gazette a notice of the expiry date of any order that imposes a surtax on any goods under subsection 55(1), section 60, subsection 63(1), 74(1) or (2), 76(1) or 77(1) of the Customs Tariff or includes any goods on the Import Control List under subsection 5(3), (3.2) or (4.1) of the Export and Import Permits Act, but no notice shall be published if
Subsection 30.03(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the effective period specified in the order and any periods during which the goods were subject to a surtax imposed by an order made under subsection 76(1) of the Customs Tariff is, in the case of motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan, five years or in the case of any other goods, three years; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the effective period specified in the order and any periods during which the goods were subject to a surtax imposed by an order made under subsection 77(1) of the Customs Tariff is four years.

Section.Text: Subsection 30.04(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Filing of request relating to extension orders

Subsection.Text: Any domestic producer of goods that are like or directly competitive with any goods that are subject to an order referred to in subsection 30.03(1), or any person or association acting on behalf of any such domestic producer, may file with the Tribunal a written request that an extension order be made under subsection 63(1), 74(7), 76(3) or 77(3) of the Customs Tariff or subsection 5(3.2) of the Export and Import Permits Act because an order continues to be necessary to prevent or remedy serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods.

Section.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnershipâhas the meaning assigned by the definition Agreement in section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act. (Accord de partenariat transpacifique global et progressiste)
CPTPP countryâmeans a party to the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership. (pays PTPGP)
CPTPP tariffâmeans any of the following tariff treatments:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Brunei Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Japan Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Malaysia Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Mexico Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive New Zealand Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Peru Tariff;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Singapore Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the Comprehensive and Progressive Vietnam Tariff. (tarif PTPGP)

Section.Text: Subparagraph 14(2)(c)(xvii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (xvii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 76(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (xviii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 77(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (xix)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 5(3), (3.2) or (4.1) of the Export and Import Permits Act.

Section.Text: Section 27 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: CPAUT refers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff.â(TAUGP)
CPBNTârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Brunei Tariff.â(TBNGP)
CPCLT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff.â(TCLGP)
CPJPT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Japan Tariff.â(TJPGP)
CPMXT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Mexico Tariff.â(TMXGP)
CPMYT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Malaysia Tariff.â(TMYGP)
CPNZT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive New Zealand Tariff.â(TNZGP)
CPPET ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Peru Tariff.â(TPEGP)
CPSGT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Singapore Tariff.â(TSGGP)
CPTPTârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff.â(TPTGP)
CPVNT ârefers to the Comprehensive and Progressive Vietnam Tariff.â(TVNGP)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 52.5:

Heading.TitleText: Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 24, goods that are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff rates of customs duty.

If âAâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPTPTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the final rate of âFreeâ.

If âFâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPTPTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced as provided in the âFâ Staging List.

If âX1â, âX2â, âX3â, âX4â, âX5â, âX6â or âX7â is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPTPTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if âX1â is set out,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on the coming into force of the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement, to three quarters of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the first year after the year in which that Agreement comes into force, to one half of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)

Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a specific rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one tenth of a cent, the rate shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of a cent.

If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of one per cent.

If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) or a rounding of rates under subsection (6) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent other than 0.5, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest percentage that divides evenly by 0.5.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Elimination of rates of less than 2%
Subsection.Text: If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that is a percentage of less than two per cent, the rate shall be further reduced to âFreeâ immediately.

The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to extend entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff to any goods that originate in a CPTPP country.

An order made under subsection (1) may, if it so provides, be retroactive and have effect in respect of a period before it is made, but no such order may have effect in respect of a period before this section comes into force.
An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must specify the date on which the order becomes effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must, if the order partially extends entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Tariff, indicate the goods to which entitlement to that Tariff is extended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may exempt goods from the conditions set out in subsection 24(1) and prescribe any conditions that apply.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 24, goods that are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff rates of customs duty.

If âAâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPAUTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the final rate of âFreeâ.

If âFâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPAUTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Australia Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced as provided in the âFâ Staging List.

Subsection.Text: If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that is a percentage of less than two per cent, the rate shall be further reduced to âFreeâ immediately.

An order made under subsection (1) may, if it so provides, be retroactive and have effect in respect of a period before it is made, but no such order may have effect in respect of a period before the first day on which the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement is in effect between Canada and Brunei.
An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must specify the date on which the order becomes effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must, if the order partially extends entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Brunei Tariff, indicate the goods to which entitlement to that Tariff is extended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may exempt goods from the conditions set out in subsection 24(1) and prescribe any conditions that apply.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 24, goods that are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff rates of customs duty.

If âAâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPCLTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the final rate of âFreeâ.

If âFâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âCPCLTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff, the Comprehensive and Progressive Chile Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced as provided in the âFâ Staging List.

Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a specific rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one tenth of a cent, the rate shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of a cent.

If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of one per cent.

If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) or a rounding of rates under subsection (6) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent other than 0.5, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest percentage that divides evenly by 0.5.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Elimination of rates of less than 2%

Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a specific rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one tenth of a cent, the rate shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of a cent.

If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of one per cent.

If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) or a rounding of rates under subsection (6) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent other than 0.5, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest percentage that divides evenly by 0.5.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Elimination of rates of less than 2%
Subsection.Text: If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that is a percentage of less than two per cent, the rate shall be further reduced to âFreeâ immediately.

The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to extend entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Malaysia Tariff to any goods that originate in a CPTPP country.

An order made under subsection (1) may, if it so provides, be retroactive and have effect in respect of a period before it is made, but no such order may have effect in respect of a period before the first day on which the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement is in effect between Canada and Malaysia.
An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must specify the date on which the order becomes effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must, if the order partially extends entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Malaysia Tariff, indicate the goods to which entitlement to that Tariff is extended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may exempt goods from the conditions set out in subsection 24(1) and prescribe any conditions that apply.

Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a specific rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one tenth of a cent, the rate shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of a cent.

If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of one per cent.

If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) or a rounding of rates under subsection (6) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent other than 0.5, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest percentage that divides evenly by 0.5.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Elimination of rates of less than 2%
Subsection.Text: If, for any goods other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.01, 87.02, 87.03, 87.04 or 87.05, a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that is a percentage of less than two per cent, the rate shall be further reduced to âFreeâ immediately.

The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, amend the schedule to extend entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Peru Tariff to any goods that originate in a CPTPP country.

An order made under subsection (1) may, if it so provides, be retroactive and have effect in respect of a period before it is made, but no such order may have effect in respect of a period before the first day on which the Comprehensive and Progressive Trans-Pacific Partnership Agreement is in effect between Canada and Peru.
An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must specify the date on which the order becomes effective;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must, if the order partially extends entitlement to the Comprehensive and Progressive Peru Tariff, indicate the goods to which entitlement to that Tariff is extended; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may exempt goods from the conditions set out in subsection 24(1) and prescribe any conditions that apply.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 24, goods that are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Singapore Tariff are entitled to the Comprehensive and Progressive Singapore Tariff rates of customs duty.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 75:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) to (5), if at any time it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, as a result of an inquiry made by the Canadian International Trade Tribunal under subsection 19.0193(2) of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act or further to a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.082) of that Act, that goods that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production, and under such conditions as to cause or threaten to cause serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspend, during the period that the order is in effect, any reduction of the rate of customs duty with respect to those goods that would otherwise be made after that time by virtue of the provision of this Act that gives effect to that CPTPP Tariff; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make those goods subject to a temporary duty, in addition to any other duty specified in this Act or in any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, at a rate set out in the order, but that rate, when added to the applicable rate of customs duty that is in effect in respect of those goods at that time, may not exceed the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff rate of customs duty that is in effect in respect of those goods at the time the order is made, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff rate of customs duty that was in effect in respect of those goods immediately before the coming into force of this subsection.

Subsection.Text: An order under subsection (1) remains in effect for the period that is specified in the order, which shall not exceed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: three years in the case of motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: two years in the case of any other goods.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, extend the period of an order made under subsection (1), if it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, as a result of an inquiry made by the Canadian International Trade Tribunal under section 30.07 of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act that the order continues to be necessary to prevent or remedy serious injury and to facilitate the adjustment of domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods. The total period of the order is not to exceed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: five years in the case of motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: three years in the case of any other goods.

Subsection.Text: An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not to be made more than once in respect of goods of any particular kind with the exception of motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may be in effect during the period beginning on the day on which this subsection comes into force and ending

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: on the third anniversary of the first day on which the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement on Trans-Pacific Partnership is in effect, if the order is in respect of goods, other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan, for which the applicable CPTPP tariff rate of customs duty is at its final rate of âFreeâ on that anniversary,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on the day on which the applicable CPTPP tariff rate of customs duty is reduced to its final rate of âFreeâ, if the order is in respect of goods, other than motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan, for which the applicable CPTPP tariff rate of customs duty is not at its final rate of âFreeâ on the anniversary referred to in subparagraph (i), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the order is in respect of motor vehicles of heading No. 87.03 imported from Japan, on the twelfth anniversary of the day on which that CPTPP tariff rate of customs duty for those goods is reduced to the final rate of âFreeâ.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Rate of duty when an order ceases to have effect
Subsection.Text: When an order made under subsection (1) ceases to have effect, the rate of customs duty applicable to the goods is the rate of customs duty that is applicable in accordance with the provision of this Act that gives effect to the CPTPP Tariff to which the goods are entitled.

Heading.TitleText: Emergency Measures for Textile and Apparel Goods Imported from CPTPP Countries

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) to (5), if at any time it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, on the basis of a report of the Minister further to a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.083) of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act or as a result of an inquiry made by the Canadian International Trade Tribunal under subparagraph 26(1)(a)(i.83) of that Act, that textile or apparel goods set out in Annex 4-A of the TPP, as defined in section 2 of the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership Implementation Act, that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to the domestic market for that good, and under such conditions as to cause serious damage or an actual threat of serious damage to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order make those goods subject to a temporary duty, in addition to any other duty specified in this Act or any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, at a rate set out in the order, but that rate, when added to the applicable rate of customs duty specified in the CPTPP Tariff that is in effect in respect of those goods at that time, may not exceed the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff rate of customs duty that is in effect in respect of those goods at the time the order is made, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff rate of customs duty that was in effect in respect of those goods immediately before the coming into force of this subsection.

Subsection.Text: An order under subsection (1) remains in effect for the period that is specified in the order, which shall not exceed two years.
The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, extend the period of an order made under subsection (1). The total period of the order is not to exceed four years.
An order made under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not to be made more than once in respect of goods of any particular kind; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may be in effect during the period beginning on the day on which this subsection comes into force and ending on the fifth anniversary of the day on which the CPTPP rate of customs duty on those goods is reduced to the final rate of âFreeâ.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Rate of duty when an order ceases to have effect
Subsection.Text: When an order made under subsection (1) ceases to have effect, the rate of customs duty applicable to the goods is the rate of customs duty that is applicable in accordance with the provision of this Act that gives effect to the CPTPP Tariff to which the goods are entitled.

Section.Text: Section 87 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Goods of tariff item No. 9971.00.00â¯ââ¯CPTPP country
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 20(2), the value for duty of goods of tariff item No. 9971.00.00 that are entitled to a CPTPP tariff is the value of the repairs or alterations made to those goods in a CPTPP country.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: Note 1: The Canada-European Union Tariff, Iceland Tariff, Korea Tariff, Norway Tariff, Switzerland-Liechtenstein Tariff and the CPTPP tariff rate of customs duty applicable to goods classified under this tariff item shall be, in respect of the value of the repair or alteration to the goods carried out only in an EU country or other CETA beneficiary, Iceland, Korea, Norway, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, or a CPTPP country, respectively, determined under section 87 of this Act, in accordance with their classification in Chapters 1 to 97.

Section.Text: Any application that is filed under section 17 of the Investment Canada Act before the day on which section 19 of this Act comes into force and in respect of which the Minister of Industry has not issued a decision before that day is deemed never to have been filed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the investment to which the application relates would have been subject to subsections 14.11(1) or (2) of the Investment Canada Act had the application been filed on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the enterprise value of the assets to which the application relates is less than the amount referred to in subsection 14.11(1) of that Act.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Coordinating Amendments and Coming into Force

Subsection.Text: In this section, other Act means the Economic Action Plan 2014 Act, No. 1.

If section 362 of the other Act comes into force before section 17 of this Act, then every reference to âtrade-nameâ in section 17 of the English version of this Act is replaced by a reference to âtrade nameâ.
If section 362 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 17 of this Act, then that section 17 is deemed to have come into force before that section 362.

If section 366 of the other Act comes into force before this Act receives royal assent, then every reference to âtrade-markâand âTrade-marksâ is replaced by a reference to âtrademarkâ, and âTrademarksâ, respectively, in the following provisions of the English version of this Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the heading before section 17; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sections 17 and 18.

Subsection.Text: If section 366 of the other Act comes into force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent, then this Act is deemed to have received royal assent before that section 366 comes into force.

Section.Text: This Act, other than section 49, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

ScheduleFormHeading.Label: SCHEDULE 1
ScheduleFormHeading.OriginatingRef: (Paragraphs 47(1)(c) and (d), (2)(c) and (d), (3)(c) and (d), (4)(c) and (d), (5)(c) and (d), (6)(c) and (d), (7)(c) and (d), (8)(c) and (d), (9)(c) and (d), (10)(c) and (d), (11)(c) and (d))
